name: Earth Analog
short_description: In this relaxing space flight adventure, you pilot the science vessel Eurisko XI on a quest through deep space. Explore fascinating, fractal-based exoplanets and uncover their mysteries. Analyze artifacts and upgrade your ship to unlock new pathways. Your mission: find humanity’s new home.
detailed_description:
In Earth Analog, you take control of the science vessel Eurisko XI on a mission through deep space. Your goal: find humanity's new home.

Your journey begins in orbit around Proxima Centauri b, a planet that feels familiar - at first. But the deeper you venture, the stranger the worlds become...

This story-rich, relaxing space flight adventure challenges you to keep your ship intact while navigating mysterious and sometimes dangerous environments. Manually fly and land your vessel, explore distant planetary systems, and venture out on foot to study alien terrain. You'll need to manage critical systems: prospect for resources, upgrade your ship, and repair damage as it happens.

Discover rare artifacts and valuable materials to unlock new paths. And can you master the mysterious Dimension Device? It's all up to you - you're completely alone out there... or are you?

For fans of: Interstellar, 2001: A Space Odyssey

Features

  • A vast universe to explore with both hand-crafted and randomly generated procedural fractal-based (living) planets and stars.

  • An intriguing single player adventure based on a story full of drama and plot twists.

  • An atmospheric and Newtonian flight model. WARNING: Newtonian flight takes some practice to get used to!

  • Solve each planet's mysteries.

  • Follow the quest path or freely roam the galaxy. The choice is yours.

  • A fully ray tracing based graphics engine bringing never before seen worlds to life.

  • HOTAS support.

  • TrackIR support.

The game is based on a unconventional render technique called ray marching, which allows it to visualize its fractal-based worlds. You need a good GPU to be able to play the game at higher resolutions! Please check if you system meets the minimal requirements.
